# Translation Tools

**Category:** Technology Domain

## Why it matters

For many Language Technologists, the input, verification, management and publishing of translated data will be a core activity of the parent organisations. As LT responds to the needs of other domains, this means that this competency is among the first competencies that should be studied.

## Target competency

> Is able to train others in the effective use of current Translation tools.

## Progression by component

_Suggested activities to reach each outcome level._

### 1.0 — Translation Process

| Level | Suggested activities |
| --- | --- |
| **Learner** | Get to know a translation team and their daily activities. |
| **Advanced Beginner** | — |
| **Practitioner** | — |
| **Trainer/Proficient** | — |
| **Expert** | — |

### 2.0 — Translation Tools

| Level | Suggested activities |
| --- | --- |
| **Learner** | Demonstrates basic knowledge of translation tools |
| **Advanced Beginner** | Can use translation tools and troubleshoot issues that arise |
| **Practitioner** | Assist in training others on the use of translation tools. |
| **Trainer/Proficient** | Prepare and lead a translation tools training event. |
| **Expert** | Prepare and teach translation tools at an LT Consultant training event. |

### 3.0 — Scripture Markup and Import

_Scripture translation will be a priority for most Language Technologists. Scripture is marked with tags that represent both structure and formatting. Specialists will need to know the intentions and tags of the current Scripture markup standards. Consultants may be asked to convert from older standards. Equipped with this knowledge, a Language Technologist will be able to prepare scripture typed in other formats for importation into translation tools. They will also be able to help teams to properly work with and translate Scripture verses._

| Level | Suggested activities |
| --- | --- |
| **Learner** | Can identify common markup notation for Scripture data based on the current standards. |
| **Advanced Beginner** | Can produce common markup for Scripture data based on the current standards. |
| **Practitioner** | Convert and import data from other formats (plain text, Word document, etc) into translation software. |
| **Trainer/Proficient** | — |
| **Expert** | Train others to convert and import Scripture data |

### 4.0 — Mobile Translation Tools

| Level | Suggested activities |
| --- | --- |
| **Learner** | Identify Mobile Translation Tools that are used in your context. |
| **Advanced Beginner** | Learn to use mobile translation tools that may be useful in your context. |
| **Practitioner** | Assist in training others on the use of mobile translation tools. |
| **Trainer/Proficient** | Train individuals to use mobile Translation Tools for appropriate tasks. |
| **Expert** | Train groups or consultants to use mobile translation tools for appropriate tasks. |

### 5.0 — Scripture Collaboration

| Level | Suggested activities |
| --- | --- |
| **Learner** | Identify the common methods of digital collaboration in Scripture translation projects. Learn about Project Plans in use in your context. |
| **Advanced Beginner** | Use the common methods of digital collaboration in Scripture translation projects. |
| **Practitioner** | Advise users in best-practices for collaboration and data safety (within the team and remotely), such as the use of Send/Receive. Assist users to configure plans and tasks in a way that helps them. |
| **Trainer/Proficient** | Train users in best-practices for collaboration and data safety (within the team and remotely), such as the use of Send/Receive, Paratext Live, and ScriptureForge |
| **Expert** | Train consultants to do remote checking sessions using all available technologies. |

### 6.0 — Translation Resource Tools

| Level | Suggested activities |
| --- | --- |
| **Learner** | Acquire and explore a translation resource package (i.e. Translator's Workplace Logos or BdT) |
| **Advanced Beginner** | Optimally configure translation resource packages for exegesis |
| **Practitioner** | Advise users to open and navigate to specific translation resources. |
| **Trainer/Proficient** | Train users to use customization, search and filters to target specific translation resources. |
| **Expert** | Train consultants to use customization, search and filters to target specific translation resources. |
